channel.title: Posts on What I read every week
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on What I read every week

item.title: What I read this week - 2023/02/17
item.description: Not in doing what you like, but in liking what you do is the secret of happiness — J.M. Barrie Articles 🔗 Higher Rates Will Lead to the Next Generation of Great Tech Startups Microsoft thinks AI can beat Google at search — CEO Satya Nadella explains why / AI is coming for your browser, your social media, and your operating system, too. References 🔗 Generative AI – The Future of Creative Work How to Summarize a Large Text with GPT-3

item.title: What I read this week - 2023/02/10
item.description: Knowledge is power. — Sir Francis Bacon Articles 🔗 Unleashing the Potential of ChatGPT in the Classroom Best practices for fine-tuning GPT-3 to classify text How ChatGPT Works: The Model Behind The Bot References 🔗 The Art of ChatGPT Prompting: A Guide to Crafting Clear and Effective Prompts Azure OpenAI Documentation Training your own ChatGPT model: A step-by-step tutorial Liftoff! How to get started with your first ML project Awesome Public Datasets Nostr NIP-05 verification with GitHub Pages Universal Clipboard Not Working Between iPhone and Mac: 9 Fixes to Try How to Protect Your Home Network with a Gigabit VPN What Server-Sent Events is - and how and when to implement it

item.title: What I read this week - 2023/02/03
item.description: The people say that all energy is only borrowed. One day you have to give it back. — Avatar The Way of Water Articles 🔗 How to contribute to a open source project Interview: What does it take to become a software architect? An advanced guide to writing prompts for Midjourney ( text-to-image ) References 🔗 Generate Images With DALL·E 2 and the OpenAI API OpenAI Cookbook ChatGPT tutorial: ChatGPT - A guide on how to use OpenAI’s new ChatGPT An online video suite for professionals
